title: Crispy Chicken Milanese with Arugula & Roasted Squash
description: HelloFresh Hall of Fame — golden-fried panko-crusted chicken cutlets served on a bed of peppery arugula with oven-roasted yellow squash, cherry tomatoes, and a bright lemon-olive oil dressing. Light, crispy, and endlessly satisfying.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Slice squash into ½-inch rounds. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast on a baking sheet for 15–18 minutes, flipping once, until golden.
Pound chicken breasts to ½-inch thickness. Set up a breading station: flour in one shallow bowl, beaten eggs in another, and panko mixed with garlic powder, parmesan, salt, and pepper in a third.
Dredge each chicken breast in flour (shake off excess), dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko mixture to coat both sides.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden brown and cooked through (165°F). Work in batches, adding more oil as needed.
Make the dressing: whisk together juice of 1 lemon, 1 tbsp olive oil, a pinch of salt, and pepper.
In a bowl, toss arugula and cherry tomatoes with the lemon dressing.
Serve chicken cutlets topped with the arugula salad and roasted squash alongside. Squeeze remaining lemon over everything.
tips: The three-step breading (flour → egg → panko) is the secret to a crust that really sticks.
Don't crowd the pan when frying — work in batches so the chicken gets crispy rather than steaming.
For a lighter version, bake the breaded chicken at 425°F for 20 minutes instead of pan-frying.
Shaved parmesan on top of the arugula adds an extra layer of flavor.
